workflow.name: Generate AI SOPs from Google Drive with GPT‑4o, Google Docs and Sheets
workflow.description:
This workflow automatically converts unstructured internal documentation into clear, actionable Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs).

Instead of manually rewriting internal knowledge, teams can upload or update documents and let AI generate consistent, step-by-step SOPs that are easy to follow and scale across the organization.

## How it works
When a new or updated document is detected in Google Drive, the workflow extracts the content and splits it into manageable sections. AI then analyzes the material, identifies implicit steps, and generates a structured SOP with clear actions, responsibilities, and outcomes.

A second AI pass refines clarity and consistency. Completed SOPs are saved to Google Docs, logged in Google Sheets, and shared with stakeholders via Slack or email.

## Who it’s for
- Operations teams
- Consulting firms
- Agencies and service businesses
- Scaling startups
- Internal enablement teams

## Requirements
- Google Drive & Docs
- Google Sheets
- OpenAI credentials
- Slack or Gmail

## Built by QuarterSmart
Created by **Hyrum Hurst**, AI Automation Engineer at **QuarterSmart**, specializing in practical, production-ready AI automation systems.

For custom SOP automation or enterprise setups:  
**hyrum@quartersmart.com**

workflow.name: Unstructured Documents to Structured SOP Converter

options.systemMessage:
You are an expert SOP (Standard Operating Procedure) generator.

Your task is to analyze unstructured internal documents and convert them into well-structured SOPs.

For the provided document text, generate a structured SOP with the following components:
1. Title: Clear, descriptive title for the SOP
2. Purpose: Brief explanation of why this procedure exists
3. Scope: Who this applies to and when it should be used
4. Steps: Detailed step-by-step instructions (numbered list)
5. Responsibilities: Who is responsible for each major step
6. References: Any related documents or resources mentioned

Ensure the output is clear, actionable, and follows standard SOP formatting conventions.

Return the structured SOP in the defined JSON format.

You are an SOP quality assurance specialist.

Your task is to review and refine the generated SOP for clarity, consistency, and completeness.

Review the provided SOP structure and:
1. Improve clarity of language and instructions
2. Ensure consistency in terminology and formatting
3. Verify all steps are actionable and specific
4. Check that responsibilities are clearly assigned
5. Validate that the scope and purpose are well-defined
6. Assess overall quality and assign a confidence score (0-1)

Return the refined SOP in the same JSON format with your improvements and confidence assessment.

parameters.message:
=

New SOP Created

A new Standard Operating Procedure has been generated and is ready for review.

SOP Details:

  • Title: {{ $json.output.title }}
  • Purpose: {{ $json.output.purpose }}
  • Scope: {{ $json.output.scope }}
  • Confidence Score: {{ $json.output.confidenceScore }}

Document Link: View SOP in Google Docs

Please review the SOP and provide any feedback to the operations team.

Best regards,
Automated SOP System
parameters.subject: =New SOP Created: {{ $json.output.title }}

parameters.content:
## Main
This workflow converts messy internal documents into clean, structured SOPs using AI.

It’s designed for teams that have knowledge scattered across docs, notes, or processes, and need repeatable, standardized procedures without manual rewriting.

Built by:
Hyrum Hurst  
AI Automation Engineer — QuarterSmart  
Contact: hyrum@quartersmart.com

## Setup
1. Connect Google Drive and Docs
2. Choose source folder for raw documents
3. Configure AI tone and SOP format
4. Set notification channels
5. Review generated SOPs before rollout

user.name: Hyrum Hurst

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.
